refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with communication quality meeting the customer-defined packet loss limit.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR focuses on a single obligation: ensuring teleoperation communication quality meets a specified packet loss limit. It is already solution-neutral and specifies what the system must achieve without prescribing how. There is no conjunction or multiple obligations to split.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the packet loss rate during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify it does not exceed the customer-defined limit (e.g., ≤ 1%).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation.
The NGHE solution shall ensure communication quality meets the customer-defined packet loss limit.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol with adaptive error correction and retransmission mechanisms to maintain packet loss within limits
Design a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet packet loss targets
Use a redundant communication path strategy to ensure packet loss stays below the threshold
Incorporate a buffering and smoothing mechanism at the receiver to compensate for packet loss and maintain teleoperation quality
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet packet loss targets

potential_sources: Tight integration between monitoring and control functions could create coupling if not modularized
Dependency on external network conditions may introduce indirect coupling
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates monitoring from control actions, supporting modularity and independence. It focuses on dynamic adjustment rather than fixed hardware or protocols, which helps maintain decoupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions can be monitored accurately and in real-time
Risk that dynamic adjustments may not be sufficient under extreme network degradation
Assumes operational policies allow for adaptive communication parameter changes
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining interfaces between monitoring and control modules to further reduce coupling and clarify responsibilities.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses network-related functionalities including monitoring and control of network parameters to ensure quality of service.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used to receive network performance data and send monitoring reports.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to adjust transmission parameters and receive network status updates.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as network quality monitoring and control logically belong here; minimal diffusion expected outside this module.
Vehicle module is more about physical vehicle functions; network control would diffuse significantly outside.
Teleoperation depends on network quality but does not own network control; moderate diffusion expected.
Safety Services focus on safety alerts, not network control; poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the network quality monitoring and control DP because it specializes in network-related functions and can encapsulate the logic and control mechanisms with minimal diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may require careful interface management to avoid tight coupling.
If network control logic overlaps with Teleoperation functions, coordination is needed to prevent duplicated functionality.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by specifying a network quality monitoring and control system that dynamically adjusts transmission parameters to meet the packet loss target, directly supporting the teleoperation requirement with customer-defined packet loss limits.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Telemetry and Network Link interfaces may require careful interface management to avoid tight coupling.
If network control logic overlaps with Teleoperation functions, coordination is needed to prevent duplicated functionality.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating strong cohesion and appropriate encapsulation of the DP. Other modules show higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the ownership boundary.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible through monitoring packet loss metrics via the Telemetry interface and ensuring packet loss remains within the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and appropriate, with well-defined interfaces (Telemetry and Network Link) supporting the realization of the FR.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules to prevent duplicated network control logic.
Establish clear dependency management and interface abstraction for Telemetry and Network Link to minimize tight coupling and diffusion risks.
Include verification procedures in the NGHE Solution module to continuously monitor packet loss and report compliance with acceptance criteria.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and appropriate containment and verification feasibility. The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and low diffusion, indicating a well-defined ownership boundary. The recommended revisions focus on documentation and interface management rather than fundamental decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to finalize the current level with these revisions rather than further decomposing, which could introduce complexity and risk.

revise.why: To explicitly document interface contracts and establish clear dependency management to prevent duplicated logic and minimize coupling, as well as include verification procedures for continuous monitoring.

assumptions: The current DP adequately addresses the FR with respect to packet loss control.
The NGHE Solution module is the appropriate implementation boundary for the DP.
open_risks: Potential tight coupling via Telemetry and Network Link interfaces.
Risk of duplicated network control logic between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.
evidence_needed: Documentation of interface contracts between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.
Verification procedures demonstrating continuous packet loss monitoring and compliance reporting.
